zh.Toledo, New Castile, Spain: 西班牙迪奧希斯 德 托萊多
zh.lifeSummary: 當Juan Perez de Toledo於1208年出生在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 托萊多時，他的父親Pedro Garcia24歲，母親Maria Dominico23歲。他和Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán至少有1個兒子。他去世於1231年在他的家鄉享年23歲。

zh-hans.Toledo, New Castile, Spain: 西班牙迪奧希斯 德 托萊多
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Juan Perez de Toledo于1208年出生在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 托萊多时，他的父亲Pedro Garcia24岁，母亲Maria Dominico23岁。他和Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán至少有1个儿子。他去世于1231年在他的家乡享年23岁。

nameMeanings.name: Perez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Ramon, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish (Pérez): patronymic from the personal name Pedro, an equivalent of Peter . This surname is also found in France. Compare De Perez .
Breton (Pérez): variant of Pérès (see Peres ).

variants.name: Parez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Pérez (see Perez ).
Czech (Pařez): nickname from pařez ‘(tree) stump’.
In some cases possibly also French (Nord), Walloon, and Flemish: variant of Paré (see Pare ) or Paret .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toledo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Jorge, Manuel, Ramon, Rafael, Juan, Antonio, Jesus, Miguel, Roberto, Carlos, Francisco.

Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from the city in central Spain, which was the capital of the Visigothic state between the 6th and 8th centuries. Its role declined for three centuries after the Muslim invasion of Spain, until it was taken as the capital of the kingdom of Castile between the 11th and 16th centuries. It was a major cultural and political center throughout the Middle Ages, and was also the home of an important Jewish community. The placename, first recorded in Latin as Toletum, is of obscure etymology, possibly connected with Toleto in Piedmont; Jewish tradition connects it with Hebrew toledot ‘generations’, but this is no more than folk etymology. Compare Toledano .

Jewish (Sephardic): also an adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the surname their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Toledano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Shlomo, Igal, Moshe, Shimon, Ehud, Shmuel, Yehuda.  Spanish Alberto, Gonzalo, Alina, Carlos, Conrado, Elena, Enrique, Evelia, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramona.  French Gabrielle, Michel.
Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name for someone from Toledo (see   Toledo  ), from an adjectival form of the placename.

nameMeanings.name: Juan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , now also used as a given name in English-speaking countries, in spite of the unfavourable associations with Don Juan, the heartless seducer of  Mozart's  opera Don Giovanni (  1788 ) and libertine hero of  Byron's  satirical epic (  1819–24 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: When Juan Perez de Toledo was born in 1208, in Diócesis de Toledo, Spain, his father, Pedro Garcia, was 24 and his mother, Maria Dominico, was 23. He had at least 1 son with Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán. He died in 1231, in his hometown, at the age of 23.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Juan Perez de Toledo est né en 1208, à Diócesis de Toledo, Espagne, son père, Pedro Garcia, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Maria Dominico, avait 23 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán. Il est décédé en 1231, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 23 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Juan Perez de Toledo 1208 in Diócesis de Toledo, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Pedro Garcia 24 und seine Mutter Maria Dominico 23. Er hatte mit Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1231 im Alter von 23 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Juan Perez de Toledo è nato il nel 1208 nel Diócesis de Toledo, Spagna, il suo padre, Pedro Garcia, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Maria Dominico, aveva 23 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán. Morì nel 1231, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 23 anni.

ja.Toledo, New Castile, Spain: スペインディoセシス ド トーレド
ja.lifeSummary: Juan Perez de Toledoが1208年にスペインディoセシス ド トーレドで生まれた時，父Pedro Garciaは24歳，母Maria Dominicoは23歳であった。彼はLeocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illánとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1231年に故郷で23歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Juan Perez de Toledo(이)가 1208년 스페인 디o스시스 데 톨도에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Pedro Garcia(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria Dominico(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1231년 그의 고향에서 23세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Juan Perez de Toledo nasceu em 1208, em Diócesis de Toledo, Espanha, seu pai, Pedro Garcia, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Maria Dominico, tinha 23 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán. Ele faleceu em 1231, em sua cidade natal, com 23 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Juan Perez de Toledo родился в 1208 г. в местности диoсесис де толедо, Испания, его отцу по имени Pedro Garcia было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Maria Dominico, 23. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán. Он умер в 1231 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 23 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Juan Perez de Toledo nació en 1208, en Diócesis de Toledo, España, su papá, Pedro Garcia, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Maria Dominico, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Leocadia ESTEBÁÑEZ -  Luna de Illán. Murió en 1231, en su pueblo, a la edad de 23 años.
